{"query":"Google non-consensual explicit deepfakes search ranking update July 2026 official announcement","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1687,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/search/search-io-2026/","title":"Google Search’s I/O 2026 updates: AI agents and more","content":"We’re also introducing a new, intelligent AI-powered Search box, marking its biggest upgrade in over 25 years.
The momentum has been incredible: Just one year after its debut, AI Mode has surpassed one billion monthly users, with queries more than doubling every quarter since launch.
Starting today, we’re upgrading Search with Gemini 3.5 Flash — our newest Flash model delivering sustained frontier performance for agents and coding — as the new default model in AI Mode for everyone globally.
Information agents will launch first for Google AI Pro & Ultra subscribers this summer.
We’re bringing the power of Google Antigravity and the agentic coding capabilities of Gemini 3.5 Flash right into Search.
Here’s a look at everything we announced at Google I/O 2026.
You may opt out at any time."},{"url":"https://mashable.com/article/google-search-nonconsensual-deepfakes","title":"Google Search tries new tactics for limiting explicit deepfakes | Mashable","content":"Google announced changes to Search designed to limit explicit deepfake imagery.
"With these changes, people can read about the impact deepfakes are having on society, rather than see pages with actual nonconsensual fake images," Higham wrote.
The ranking updates have already decreased exposure to explicit image results on deepfake searches by 70 percent, according to Higham.
Google is also aiming to downrank explicit deepfake content, though Higham noted that it can be difficult to distinguish between content that is real and consensual, such as an actor's nude scenes, and material generated by artificial intelligence, without an actor's consent.
Suz Chambers, Director of Google Creative Lab, presented the new tool at Google I/O 2026."},{"url":"https://onward.justia.com/googles-latest-search-algorithm-update-targets-explicit-deepfakes/","title":"Google’s Latest Search Algorithm Update Targets Explicit Deepfakes","content":"The new search algorithm updates make it easier to remove harmful content and prevent it from appearing in search results."},{"url":"https://momenticmarketing.com/google-updates/july-2024-explicit-fake-content-update","title":"Google Explicit Fake Content Updates: What You Need to Know | Momentic","content":"Below you'll get tips on what to look for, how to respond on your website, and helpful resources to improve your website's SEO and UX as it relates specifically to this Google update.
News articles show up first for name searches.
Those with non-consensual explicit content, especially AI-generated."},{"url":"https://www.searchenginejournal.com/google-search-algorithm-update-targeting-explicit-deepfakes/523478/","title":"Google Search Algorithm Update Targeting Explicit Deepfakes","content":"Google has modified its systems to streamline removing non-consensual explicit fake content from search results.
A new scanning system has been implemented to identify and remove duplicate images once an original has been successfully removed from search results.
The search algorithm has been adjusted to potentially reduce the visibility of explicit fake content in many searches.
Websites with numerous pages removed due to fake explicit imagery may see changes in their overall search rankings.
Google mentions plans to collaborate with industry partners and experts to address this challenge more broadly.
This algorithm update represents Google’s latest effort to adapt its search functionality in response to evolving digital content challenges.
As reported by Google, the update has led to a significant reduction in exposure to explicit image results for specific queries, with over a 70% decrease for targeted searches."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Google non-consensual explicit deepfakes search ranking update July 2026 official announcement","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://mashable.com/article/google-search-nonconsensual-deepfakes","title":"Google Search tries new tactics for limiting explicit deepfakes | Mashable","content":"The explosion of nonconsensual deepfake imagery online in the past year, particularly of female celebrities, has presented a difficult challenge for search engines. Google is trying to tackle that problem with an update to its ranking systems, the company announced in a blog post\"). Google product manager Emma Higham wrote in the post that the ranking updates are designed to lower explicit fake content for many searches. Google is also aiming to downrank explicit deepfake content, though Higham noted that it can be difficult to distinguish between content that is real and consensual, such as an actor's nude scenes, and material generated by artificial intelligence, without an actor's consent. Sites with a high volume of removals for fake explicit imagery will now be demoted in Search. When a victim is able to remove deepfakes of themselves from Google Search, the company's systems will aim to filter all related results on similar searches about them, and scan and remove duplicates of that imagery.","score":0.7686816,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.searchenginejournal.com/google-search-algorithm-update-targeting-explicit-deepfakes/523478","title":"Google Search Algorithm Update Targeting Explicit Deepfakes","content":"Prove What's Moving Your AI Search Results. Get the Rundown on what needs to change in your content ops. Google updates search algorithm to combat explicit deepfakes, improving content removal and adjusting rankings to protect users from non-consensual imagery. Google has launched a new update to its search algorithm aimed at addressing non-consensual explicit content, mainly artificially generated images and videos known as “deepfakes.”. Google has modified its systems to streamline removing non-consensual explicit fake content from search results. The search algorithm has been adjusted to potentially reduce the visibility of explicit fake content in many searches. ### How does the new content removal process work in Google’s update? ### What are the key changes in Google’s ranking system due to the update? As reported by Google, the update has led to a significant reduction in exposure to explicit image results for specific queries, with over a 70% decrease for targeted searches.","score":0.7476348,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.nbcnews.com/tech/tech-news/google-announces-news-steps-combat-sexually-explicit-deepfakes-rcna164560","title":"Google announces news steps to combat sexually explicit deepfakes","content":"After a staggering increase in the number of fake pornographic videos and images uploaded online in the last several years, Google on Wednesday announced new measures to assist victims and reduce the prominence of deepfakes in top search results. The search engine also committed to taking steps to derank websites that frequently host the nonconsensual sexually explicit fake videos — also known as deepfakes — meaning that they may appear lower in search results. The platform has also included links to tools used to create nonconsensual sexually explicit deepfakes in top results. In its announcement Wednesday, Google said it will aim to filter explicit content from similar searches after victims successfully request the removal of explicit nonconsensual fake imagery through an online form. Google also said that it will scan for and remove duplicates of nonconsensual sexually explicit deepfake images from search results after images are successfully flagged and taken down.","score":0.70214266,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://blog.google/products-and-platforms/products/search/google-search-explicit-deep-fake-content-update","title":"How Google Search is addressing explicit fake content","content":"How we're addressing explicit fake content in Search. We’re making improvements to help people affected by non-consensual sexually explicit fake content, often known as “deepfakes.”. These include: updates to our removal processes to make it easier for people to remove this content from Search and updates to our ranking systems to keep this type of content from appearing high up in Search results. For many years, people have been able to request the removal of non-consensual fake explicit imagery from Search under our policies. When someone successfully requests the removal of explicit non-consensual fake content featuring them from Search, Google’s systems will also aim to filter all explicit results on similar searches about them. So in addition to improving our processes for reporting and removing this content, we are updating our ranking systems for queries where there’s a higher risk of explicit fake content appearing in Search.","score":0.69795954,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://timesofindia.indiatimes.com/technology/tech-news/google-enhances-measures-against-non-consensual-explicit-deepfakes/articleshow/112194375.cms","title":"Google enhances measures against non-consensual ...","content":"Aug 1, 2024 — Google has announced updates to its policies and systems aimed at combating non-consensual explicit deepfake content.Read more","score":0.50886196,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.51,"request_id":"9fa6efde-0dde-4c4b-83c5-badfba3c19ed"}
